mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher expected corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate a demand-supply expansion loop where rising consumer spending validates production growth, jointly forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns only during accommodative monetary policy regimes when financing costs are low; when FEDFUNDS is elevated, the same production growth signal is neutralized by discount rate effects.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, month-over-month INDPRO growth does not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly higher than in high-rate regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation pressures real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate combined with rising housing starts signals that labor market tightness is translating into durable goods demand and residential investment, jointly forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with residential expansion
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE is falling and HOUST is rising fail to generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing output and inventory drawdowns, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
